questions.prompt: My sister ______ a new bicycle.
We ______ to the park every Sunday morning.
There is ______ new cycle path beside the river.
The air ______ much cleaner outside the city.
I ride my bicycle to college ______ the morning.
Yesterday we ______ twenty kilometres along the coast.
Cycling is ______ than driving in the city centre.
Next month the council ______ build a new cycle bridge.
There aren't ______ bicycle racks outside the station.
You ______ ride on the pavement — it is dangerous for people walking.
I ______ this bicycle since I started at college.
If the weather is dry on Saturday, we ______ ride to the lake.
The cycle lane on Bridge Street ______ resurfaced last month.
If I had a lighter bicycle, I ______ climb these hills much more easily.
She ______ for the charity ride since February.
We ______ cycle through the woods, but the path is closed now.
If we ______ the forecast, we would not have been caught in the storm.
The coach said that the club ______ a new route the following week.
By the time we reached the café, the rain ______ already stopped.
I wish I ______ a repair kit before setting off this morning.
The scheme ______ to have cut traffic in the centre by a fifth.
Not only ______ the ride raise money, but it also brought the neighbourhood together.
If he had bought a stronger lock, his bicycle ______ in the shed now.
______ impressed the organisers most was the number of families who took part.
You ______ have told me the path was flooded — I rode straight into it!
Choose the word from the bank that has the same meaning as each word below.
Match each cycling word with its definition.

bank: a hard hat worn to protect the head
a small hole in a tyre that lets the air escape
the part you push with your foot to move a bicycle
the part you use to slow down or stop
a metal chain used to fasten a bicycle to a rack
a lamp fixed to the front of a bicycle
a narrow path reserved for people on foot
a bag carried on the back while riding
a machine that measures the speed of a vehicle

answers: a hard hat worn to protect the head
a small hole in a tyre that lets the air escape
the part you push with your foot to move a bicycle
the part you use to slow down or stop

questions.prompt: Choose the word from the bank that best completes each sentence.

items.text: Cycling to college every day can ______ your risk of heart disease.
A volunteer mechanic will ______ any broken chain free of charge on Saturday.
Most riders ______ the ring road, because the traffic there is so heavy.
Regular exercise can ______ your sleep as well as your mood.
The council hopes the new lanes will ______ more people to leave their cars at home.

questions.prompt: Choose the word from the bank that goes with each verb to make a common expression.
Match each word about transport and the environment with the phrase that explains it.

bank: harmful gases released into the air by vehicles
a situation in which roads are blocked by too much traffic
able to continue for a long time without damaging the environment
the physical energy that lets you keep going for a long time
the basic roads, bridges and systems that a city needs in order to work
a charge paid by drivers who enter the city centre
the noise made by engines in a busy street
a machine that cleans the air inside a building
the highest speed at which a vehicle is allowed to travel
a period during which public transport is free of charge

answers: harmful gases released into the air by vehicles
a situation in which roads are blocked by too much traffic
able to continue for a long time without damaging the environment
the physical energy that lets you keep going for a long time
the basic roads, bridges and systems that a city needs in order to work
